skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating and updating AI skills with structured workflows, resource planning, packaging guidance, and iteration practices.
skill.summary: Create and refine AI skills with practical structure, resources, validation, and iteration guidance.
skill.author: DavidDworetzky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium issues, but most are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, example paths, and ordinary words such as description. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md. The remaining concern is guidance to run relative scripts, which could execute the wrong local file if used outside a trusted skill tooling context.

medium_findings.title: Relative Script Execution Guidance Requires Trust Boundary
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run relative commands such as scripts/init_skill.py and scripts/package_skill.py. This is legitimate skill-creation workflow guidance, but the audited package contains only SKILL.md, so an agent could execute a workspace-local script with the same path if context is unclear.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain explicit script execution guidance, and the package does not include those scripts. The workflow is legitimate, so the risk depends on the runtime directory and trust context.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and inline code spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples and file names in SKILL.md, not executable source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is all Markdown prose, inline code, or fenced command examples. No Ruby source, shell interpolation, or executable implementation is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Plain Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography hits occur in prose and metadata, including words such as description and documentation text. No cryptographic API, hashing function, encryption setting, or secret handling appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation at the cited lines. The matches are ordinary skill documentation terms, so the static blocker classification is not supported.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged documentation about including grep search patterns and guidance about asking concise questions. These lines do not collect system information, enumerate files, or transmit host details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language guidance inside SKILL.md. No command sequence or host reconnaissance behavior is present at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Script Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows relative script commands for initialization and packaging. Agents should resolve these commands to trusted bundled tooling before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit, but they are documentation for expected skill tooling rather than hidden code. The risk is contextual and can be managed with path verification.

content.user_title: Create AI Skills With Structured Guidance
content.value_statement: Building reusable AI skills is hard when structure, resources, and validation are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical skill design, packaging, and iteration steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides planning for scripts, references, and assets in a skill package.
Describes progressive disclosure for keeping skill instructions concise.
Provides a step-by-step workflow for creating, editing, packaging, and iterating on skills.
Gives concrete examples for PDF, frontend app, and BigQuery style skills.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include executable helper scripts.
Several instructions are written for Claude-specific environments.
It does not validate a finished skill by itself.
Relative script commands require trusted tooling and correct working paths.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Define a reusable skill package for repeated team workflows, including instructions, resources, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review a current skill and identify clearer triggers, leaner instructions, and better resource organization.

use_cases.title: Plan Marketplace-Ready Skill Content
use_cases.description: Structure a skill so users can understand its purpose, required files, and expected usage pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple new skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a skill plan for helping an AI agent summarize weekly product updates. Include the skill name, description, core instructions, and needed resources.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the reusable resources for a document review skill. Decide whether it needs scripts, references, assets, or only SKILL.md instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill and suggest changes to improve triggers, progressive disclosure, resource references, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Publication
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this skill concept into a publishable package plan. Include concise instructions, required files, validation criteria, risks, and iteration tests.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for rotating PDF files.
output: A recommendation to include a reusable PDF rotation script.
Validation steps for testing the script on sample files.

output_examples.input: Improve my frontend app builder skill.
output: Clearer trigger language for app-building requests.
A plan to move framework details into reference files.
A checklist for testing generated projects.
Notes about removing placeholder resources before release.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused on essential workflow steps and move detailed references into separate files.
Use scripts only when deterministic behavior or repeated implementation is needed.
Validate the skill on realistic tasks before sharing it with users.
anti_patterns: Do not include unrelated README files, changelogs, or process notes in the skill package.
Do not put every detail in SKILL.md when references can be loaded only when needed.
Do not run relative scripts unless their trusted source and path are clear.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create structured AI skills with instructions, resources, validation steps, and iteration guidance.

faq.question: Does it include working scripts?
faq.answer: No. The audited package contains documentation only and references scripts as expected external tooling.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Codex, but some wording in the source skill is Claude-specific.

faq.question: What files should a basic skill include?
faq.answer: A basic skill needs SKILL.md. Scripts, references, and assets are optional when they support the workflow.

faq.question: How does it keep skills concise?
faq.answer: It recommends progressive disclosure, where only essential instructions stay in SKILL.md and details move into resources.

faq.question: What is the main security warning?
faq.answer: Relative script commands should only be run after confirming the script path and source are trusted.
